growth_records.cite: Huimin Ye et al. Ecophysiology and interactions of a taurine-respiring bacterium in the mouse gut. Nature Communications (2023). DOI:10.1038/s41467-023-41008-z. [PMC10507020]
growth_records.snippet: Complete utilization of taurine as electron acceptor in modified Desulfovibrio liquid medium with electron donors lactate and pyruvate in excess resulted in production of nearly quantitative amounts of H2S and excess acetate (Fig. 1d).
growth_records.notes: Genuine experimental anaerobic prokaryote growth condition (taurine respiration), but NO numeric specific rates, growth rate (mu), doubling time, or biomass (gDW) are stated in the article text. The time-course of taurine, sulfide and acetate is shown only graphically in Fig. 1d, with underlying numbers in a Source Data file not accessible to this curation. No rate could be computed without inventing numbers, so all rate fields are null. Qualitative experimental facts captured: taurine (10 mmol/l) completely consumed as electron acceptor; near-stoichiometric H2S production; excess acetate produced; NH3 released but not measured; pyruvate not measured. Additional stated (qualitative) physiology, not turned into records for lack of numbers: growth rate and final yield increased with taurine at 20 and 40 mmol/l vs 10 mmol/l, inhibited at >=60 mmol/l; lower rate/yield when pyruvate omitted; formate also usable as e-donor (extended lag, lower yield); 3-sulfolactate and thiosulfate usable as alternative e-acceptors (10 mmol/l, with lactate+pyruvate); no growth on DHPS, isethionate, cysteate, sulfate, or sulfite; grew equally at pH 6-8.5, optimum temperature 27-32 C with reduced yield at 37 and 42 C; acetate (20 mmol/l) tested as carbon source with H2. Other three papers in this batch (PMC10505187 CRISPRi promoter-response study in E. coli across minimal glucose/minimal glycerol/LB, PMC10516654 MrpR structural biology in B. subtilis, PMC10523453 in-vivo CRISPR knockdown of gut Bacteroides in gnotobiotic mice) report NO experimental uptake/secretion/growth rates and yield zero records.
